name: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2017
description: International standard for software lifecycle processes. Establishes a common framework for software lifecycle processes with well-defined terminology that can be referenced by the software industry. Covers agreement, organizational project-enabling, technical management, and technical processes.
license_note: Metadata only. No ISO copyrighted text reproduced. Process descriptions are original summaries.

items.title: Acquisition process
items.description: Defines the activities for obtaining a system, software product, or software service from a supplier. Covers need identification, supplier selection, contract preparation, and acceptance of delivered products or services.
items.purpose: Obtain a system, software product, or software service that satisfies the acquirer's needs.

items.title: Supply process
items.description: Defines the activities for providing a system, software product, or software service to an acquirer. Covers proposal preparation, contract execution, product delivery, and support arrangements.
items.purpose: Provide a system, software product, or software service to the acquirer.

items.title: Life cycle model management process
items.description: Defines, maintains, and assures availability of policies, life cycle processes, life cycle models, and procedures for use by the organization. Establishes the organizational process assets.
items.purpose: Manage organizational life cycle models and process assets.

items.title: Infrastructure management process
items.description: Provides the infrastructure and services to projects to support organization and project objectives throughout the life cycle. Covers development environments, tools, computing resources, and facilities.
items.purpose: Provide and maintain the infrastructure needed to support life cycle processes.

items.title: Portfolio management process
items.description: Initiates and sustains necessary, sufficient, and suitable projects in order to meet the strategic objectives of the organization. Prioritizes and authorizes projects based on strategic alignment and resource availability.
items.purpose: Manage the project portfolio to meet organizational strategic objectives.

items.title: Human resource management process
items.description: Provides the organization with necessary human resources and maintains their competencies consistent with business needs. Covers skills assessment, training, and career development.
items.purpose: Provide and maintain competent human resources for software lifecycle activities.

items.title: Quality management process
items.description: Assures that products, services, and implementations of life cycle processes meet organizational quality objectives and achieve customer satisfaction. Defines quality policies, objectives, and evaluation criteria.
items.purpose: Assure that products and services meet quality objectives and customer requirements.

items.title: Knowledge management process
items.description: Creates, maintains, and makes available the knowledge assets of the organization. Covers knowledge capture, sharing, reuse, and organizational learning from project experiences.
items.purpose: Create and manage organizational knowledge assets for reuse.

items.title: Project planning process
items.description: Produces and coordinates effective and workable plans. Defines scope, identifies tasks, estimates effort and resources, schedules activities, and establishes project controls.
items.purpose: Produce effective project plans that guide project execution.

items.title: Project assessment and control process
items.description: Determines the status of the project and ensures that the project performs according to plans and schedules. Takes corrective action when deviations are detected.
items.purpose: Assess project progress and take corrective actions when needed.

items.title: Decision management process
items.description: Selects the most beneficial course of action where alternatives exist. Provides a structured decision-making approach for technical and management decisions using defined criteria and evaluation methods.
items.purpose: Support informed decision making through structured evaluation of alternatives.

items.title: Risk management process
items.description: Identifies, analyzes, treats, and monitors risks continuously through the project lifecycle. Covers risk identification, probability and impact assessment, response planning, and risk monitoring.
items.purpose: Identify, analyze, and manage project risks throughout the lifecycle.

items.title: Configuration management process
items.description: Establishes and maintains the integrity of all identified outputs of a project or process and makes them available to concerned parties. Covers configuration identification, control, status accounting, and auditing.
items.purpose: Maintain the integrity of project outputs through configuration control.

items.title: Information management process
items.description: Provides relevant, timely, complete, valid, and confidential information to designated parties during and after the system lifecycle. Manages information items and records.
items.purpose: Manage information items and records throughout the lifecycle.

items.title: Measurement process
items.description: Collects, analyzes, and reports data relating to the products developed and processes implemented within the organization to support effective management and demonstrate the quality of processes and products.
items.purpose: Collect and analyze measurement data to support management and quality assurance.

items.title: Quality assurance process
items.description: Provides assurance that work products and processes comply with predefined provisions and plans. Covers quality evaluations, audits, and assessments to verify conformance with quality requirements.
items.purpose: Ensure work products and processes comply with quality requirements.

items.title: Business or mission analysis process
items.description: Defines the business or mission problem or opportunity, characterizes the solution space, and determines a preferred solution approach. Covers stakeholder identification, needs analysis, and feasibility assessment.
items.purpose: Define the business problem and determine a preferred solution approach.

items.title: Stakeholder needs and requirements definition process
items.description: Defines the stakeholder requirements for a system that can provide the capabilities needed by users and other stakeholders in a defined environment. Transforms stakeholder needs into measurable requirements.
items.purpose: Define stakeholder requirements based on identified needs.

items.title: System/software requirements definition process
items.description: Transforms the stakeholder requirements into a set of system or software requirements that will guide system design and implementation. Specifies functional, performance, interface, and constraint requirements.
items.purpose: Transform stakeholder requirements into system/software requirements.

items.title: Architecture definition process
items.description: Generates system or software architecture alternatives, selects one or more that address stakeholder concerns and system requirements, and expresses this in a consistent set of views and architecture descriptions.
items.purpose: Define the system architecture that addresses requirements and stakeholder concerns.

items.title: Design definition process
items.description: Provides sufficient detailed data and information about the system and its elements to enable implementation consistent with architectural entities. Covers detailed design of software components, interfaces, and data structures.
items.purpose: Create detailed design sufficient to enable implementation.

items.title: Implementation process
items.description: Realizes a specified system element, producing code, documentation, and associated artifacts. Covers coding, unit construction, and preparation of implementation artifacts.
items.purpose: Realize the system element through coding and construction.

items.title: Integration process
items.description: Assembles a system that is consistent with the architecture and design. Integrates software components, subsystems, and system elements together and verifies that integrated assemblies meet interface requirements.
items.purpose: Integrate system elements and verify the integrated assembly.

items.title: Verification process
items.description: Confirms that specified design requirements are fulfilled by the system. Provides objective evidence that the outputs of a lifecycle activity meet specified requirements. Covers reviews, inspections, testing, and analysis.
items.purpose: Confirm that system outputs fulfill specified requirements.

items.title: Transition process
items.description: Establishes a capability to provide services specified by stakeholder requirements in the operational environment. Covers installation, activation, and transition from existing to new systems.
items.purpose: Transition the system into operational use.

items.title: Validation process
items.description: Provides objective evidence that the system when in use fulfills its business or mission objectives and stakeholder requirements, achieving its intended use in its intended operational environment.
items.purpose: Validate that the system fulfills its intended use and stakeholder requirements.

items.title: Operation process
items.description: Uses the system to deliver its services in the operational environment. Covers operational procedures, monitoring of system performance, and management of the operational system.
items.purpose: Operate the system to deliver its intended services.

items.title: Maintenance process
items.description: Sustains the capability of the system to provide a service by modifying the system to correct defects, adapt to changed environments, or improve performance and other attributes. Covers corrective, adaptive, perfective, and preventive maintenance.
items.purpose: Maintain the system's capability through modifications and corrections.

items.title: Disposal process
items.description: Ends the existence of a system element or system when no longer needed. Covers decommissioning, data archiving, environmentally responsible disposal, and transition planning for affected users.
items.purpose: Safely dispose of or decommission systems that are no longer needed.
